summary: This cycle says major labs are packaging agent workflows for broader adoption (Gemini Spark, Codex mobile), Codex keeps expanding — mobile, enterprise, and deeper agent-loop engineering. The deeper pattern is that agentic AI is being compressed, packaged, and made more inspectable at the same time.

highlights.title: Building self-improving tax agents with Codex
highlights.summary: Building self-improving tax agents with Codex
highlights.why: Agent UX is moving beyond chat boxes — interface design is becoming part of the research frontier.

highlights.title: How Virgin Atlantic ships faster with Codex
highlights.summary: How Virgin Atlantic ships faster with Codex
highlights.why: The strongest practical signal right now is still better tooling around how models call, sequence, and recover from actions.

highlights.title: Gemini Omni
highlights.why: Major lab launches still reshape the practical design space for agent builders almost overnight.

highlights.title: Show HN: Pablo – a Chrome extension that copies UI from any website
highlights.summary: Pablo is a Chrome extension that copies the HTML and CSS behind any element you hover. It captures computed styles, fonts (with @font-face and Google Fonts links), CSS keyframes, and animation props from GSAP and Framer Motion. The output is structured so it pastes cleanly into Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex when you want to rebuild a component in your own stack. Manifest v3, no host permissions, no backend. Free. https://usepablo.dev Happy to answer questions about how the extraction works, and would love to hear about sites where the output breaks.
highlights.why: One of the largest enterprise agent deployments yet — SAP embedding Claude as a primary reasoning engine across S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and Ariba via MCP signals that enterprise agent workflows are moving into production at scale.

highlights.title: google-gemini/gemini-cli
highlights.summary: An open-source AI agent that brings the power of Gemini directly into your terminal.
highlights.why: The local/open-weight ecosystem keeps making serious agent workflows cheaper to run, inspect, and iterate.

highlights.title: LearningCircuit/local-deep-research
highlights.summary: ~95% on SimpleQA (e.g. Qwen3.6-27B on a 3090). Supports all local and cloud LLMs (llama.cpp, Ollama, Google, ...). 10+ search engines - arXiv, PubMed, your private documents. Everything Local & Encrypted.
highlights.why: The local/open-weight ecosystem keeps making serious agent workflows cheaper to run, inspect, and iterate.

items.title: Building a safe, effective sandbox to enable Codex on Windows
items.why: OpenAI is turning coding agents into a more deployable systems product. The sandbox angle matters because agent adoption increasingly depends on guardrails, not raw model quality alone.

items.title: MinishLab/semble
items.summary: Fast and Accurate Code Search for Agents. Uses ~98% fewer tokens than grep+read
items.why: This is a strong agent-engineering efficiency signal: giving coding agents semantic code search instead of grep-based token waste directly reduces cost and improves latency on every agent loop.

items.title: antoinezambelli/forge
items.summary: A Python framework for self-hosted LLM tool-calling and multi-step agentic workflows
items.why: The strongest practical signal right now is still better tooling around how models call, sequence, and recover from actions.

tangentRadar.whyItMatters: The local/open-weight ecosystem keeps making serious agent workflows cheaper to run, inspect, and iterate.
The comment volume makes this a good proxy for what technically engaged builders think is worth paying attention to.

experimentQueue.title: Audit one deployed enterprise agent workflow
experimentQueue.why: Codex-on-Dell and SAP-with-Claude are production-scale agent deployments — worth understanding the real friction points.
experimentQueue.prompt: Take one enterprise agent deployment case study, map the hidden assumptions, and note where trust, permissions, and failure recovery still feel weak.

experimentQueue.title: Instrument one agent with better traces
experimentQueue.why: Even when state machines are not explicit, better traces are still the easiest way to understand why an agent drifted or stalled.
experimentQueue.prompt: Log tool calls, retries, and summaries for one real task, then inspect which step actually predicts success or failure.

experimentQueue.title: Stress-test one agent across model families
experimentQueue.why: The model layer is changing quickly enough that the same harness can behave very differently across providers and sizes.
experimentQueue.prompt: Replay the same agent task on one hosted frontier model and one smaller open model, then compare tool-selection behavior, not just answer quality.

method: Targeted Hacker News searches for high-signal agentic AI posts from the last 7 days
Reddit weekly top-post scan in LocalLLaMA and ClaudeAI filtered for practical agent-building signals
Hugging Face model search filtered toward fresh open-weight agent, deep-research, and Qwen-related releases
Major-lab RSS checks plus official-domain matches surfaced through Hacker News
GitHub repo and release scans for fresh agent tooling, dashboards, browsers, workspaces, and coding-agent updates
